blocks.content: Davenport arrived at eight o'clock exactly.
He came with a second person — Detective Serrano, forties, dark coat, notepad already open. He introduced her, caught himself on the name, glanced at Nadia, moved on. Nobody laughed.
They sat in the living room. All five on one side. Davenport and Serrano on the other. The coffee table between them like a line nobody had crossed yet.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Thank you for staying. I'll be direct.
He let that land first. Then:

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: As of last night, this is a formal homicide investigation.
The word homicide entered the room and the room changed.
Sophie's hand came up to her mouth. Not dramatically — just the body's instinct, the small protective gesture of someone trying to hold something in.
Cade's eyes closed for exactly two seconds. When they opened he was looking at the floor.
Dean went very still — the stillness of someone whose brain has already moved into a different mode, sorting, planning, looking for the thing to do next.
Wren didn't move at all. But her hands, which had been loose in her lap, slowly pressed flat against her thighs.
Nadia looked at Davenport. She didn't look away. Her face went through something — a flicker, brief and unnameable — and then it was composed again. Still. The way still is when someone chooses it.
Five people. Five completely different ways of absorbing the same word.
